machinery_genes.quote: Besides the known lspDE, lspF, and lspGHIJK, we found two loci, designated lspC and lspLM, that encode proteins with greatest homology (49, 43, and 52% similarity) with Aeromonas hydrophila ExeC, Erwinia chrysanthemi OutL, and A. hydrophila ExeM, respectively
machinery_genes.note: Rossier, Starkenburg & Cianciotto 2004. LspC named as a type II secretion gene of the L. pneumophila Lsp system; homology to ExeC (= GspC inner-membrane platform family). Encoded outside the lspD-K range of the sys_instance_label but documented as part of the same Lsp T2SS.

machinery_genes.quote: In one locus, lspD is predicted to encode the outer membrane secretin, and lspE is predicted to encode the ATPase of the system.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001, founding apparatus paper (L. pneumophila strain 130b). lspDE characterized by mutagenesis; lspDE/lspG mutants lose secretion of six PilD-dependent enzymatic activities.

machinery_genes.quote: In one locus, lspD is predicted to encode the outer membrane secretin, and lspE is predicted to encode the ATPase of the system.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001. lspE = secretion ATPase of the Lsp T2SS, co-transcribed with lspD; confirmed via lspDE mutant phenotype.

machinery_genes.quote: lspF, which encodes the inner membrane protein LspF
machinery_genes.note: Rossier, Starkenburg & Cianciotto 2004. LspF = GspF-family integral IM platform component; lspF mutant analyzed and defective in vivo (A/J mouse lung) and for secreted enzyme activities.

machinery_genes.quote: The other locus, lspFGHIJK, is predicted to encode the pseudopilins, which, in other bacteria, are directly processed by PilD.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001. lspG is the first of the lspFGHIJK pseudopilin cluster (GspG major pseudopilin by position); lspG mutant directly characterized and loses six PilD-dependent secreted activities.

machinery_genes.quote: The other locus, lspFGHIJK, is predicted to encode the pseudopilins, which, in other bacteria, are directly processed by PilD.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001; named within the lspFGHIJK pseudopilin operon. GspH minor pseudopilin role from apparatus brief vocabulary. Confirmed named as pseudopilin LspGHIJK in Rossier et al 2004 (PMID 14688110).

machinery_genes.quote: The other locus, lspFGHIJK, is predicted to encode the pseudopilins, which, in other bacteria, are directly processed by PilD.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001; named within the lspFGHIJK pseudopilin operon. GspI minor pseudopilin role from apparatus brief. Confirmed named as pseudopilin LspGHIJK in Rossier et al 2004 (PMID 14688110).

machinery_genes.quote: The other locus, lspFGHIJK, is predicted to encode the pseudopilins, which, in other bacteria, are directly processed by PilD.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001; named within the lspFGHIJK pseudopilin operon. GspJ minor pseudopilin role from apparatus brief. Confirmed named as pseudopilin LspGHIJK in Rossier et al 2004 (PMID 14688110).

machinery_genes.quote: The other locus, lspFGHIJK, is predicted to encode the pseudopilins, which, in other bacteria, are directly processed by PilD.
machinery_genes.note: Rossier & Cianciotto 2001; named within the lspFGHIJK pseudopilin operon, terminal gene of the lspD-K range of the sys_instance_label. GspK minor pseudopilin role from apparatus brief. Confirmed named as pseudopilin LspGHIJK in Rossier et al 2004 (PMID 14688110).

machinery_genes.quote: Besides the known lspDE, lspF, and lspGHIJK, we found two loci, designated lspC and lspLM, that encode proteins with greatest homology (49, 43, and 52% similarity) with Aeromonas hydrophila ExeC, Erwinia chrysanthemi OutL, and A. hydrophila ExeM, respectively
machinery_genes.note: Rossier et al 2004. LspL named as a type II secretion gene of the Lsp system; greatest homology to Erwinia chrysanthemi OutL (= GspL inner-membrane platform family that binds the ATPase). Encoded in the lspLM locus, outside the lspD-K label range but part of the same Lsp T2SS.

machinery_genes.quote: Besides the known lspDE, lspF, and lspGHIJK, we found two loci, designated lspC and lspLM, that encode proteins with greatest homology (49, 43, and 52% similarity) with Aeromonas hydrophila ExeC, Erwinia chrysanthemi OutL, and A. hydrophila ExeM, respectively
machinery_genes.note: Rossier et al 2004. LspM named as a type II secretion gene of the Lsp system; greatest homology to Aeromonas hydrophila ExeM (= GspM inner-membrane platform family). Co-encoded with lspL in the lspLM locus.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures pseudopilins; shared with type IV pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: PilD prepilin peptidase is necessary for the cleavage and methylation of pilins and pseudopilins
machinery_genes.note: Rossier et al 2004. PilD = the L. pneumophila prepilin peptidase (GspO family), shared with the type IV pilus system, required for L. pneumophila type II (Lsp) secretion; processes the LspGHIJK pseudopilins. Encoded outside the lsp operons but functionally part of the Lsp apparatus.

notes: The Lsp (Legionella secretion pathway) system is the single chromosomal T2SS of Legionella pneumophila, characterized by the Cianciotto lab. The founding apparatus paper (Rossier & Cianciotto 2001, PMID 11254562, strain 130b) constructed and characterized lspDE (secretin LspD + ATPase LspE) and lspFGHIJK (pseudopilins, plus GspF-family LspF) mutants, all PilD-dependent. The follow-up genome-survey paper (Rossier, Starkenburg & Cianciotto 2004, PMID 14688110, also strain 130b) explicitly names each gene/product (outer membrane secretin LspD, ATPase LspE, inner membrane protein LspF, pseudopilins LspGHIJK) and reports two further loci, lspC (homolog of Aeromonas ExeC = GspC) and lspLM (homologs of OutL = GspL and ExeM = GspM), giving a gene set comparable to other gram-negative T2SS. Strain transfer: the apparatus was characterized in strain 130b; the benchmark instance is Philadelphia-1 (NC_002942.5), which carries the orthologous single Lsp system (lpg locus tags), so the gene-level apparatus is directly transferable. The sys_instance_label range lspD-K covers lspDE + lspFGHIJK; I additionally include lspC, lspLM (GspC/L/M platform components named in the same papers as part of the same system) and PilD (prepilin peptidase, GspO, shared with the T4 pilus), per the apparatus brief instruction to treat the whole named system and to count PilD/GspO when tied to this T2SS. Full apparatus coverage: secretin (LspD), ATPase (LspE), IM platform (LspC, LspF, LspL, LspM), major+minor pseudopilins (LspG-LspK), prepilin peptidase (PilD). Deliberately excluded: the secreted substrates/effectors (proteases, lipases, phospholipases, acid phosphatase, RNase, and the curated lpg effectors), the upstream Sec/Tat translocon (general IM transport, not T2SS apparatus), and the type IV pilus structural genes (pilEL, pilQ secretin, pspA) which the 2004 paper shows are NOT required for type II secretion. No GspN/GspS accessory gene is described for the Lsp system. Locus_tags were not resolved (separate scripted step); gene names only as required.
